transcript:
[00:02] Sarah Johnson: Thanks for making time today. I wanted to do our Q1 review and make sure we're aligned heading into Q2.
[00:28] Marcus Webb: Sure. I'll be honest — it's been a bit of a frustrating quarter on our end.
[00:41] Sarah Johnson: I appreciate you saying that. Can you tell me more about what's been frustrating?
[00:55] Marcus Webb: A few things. First, the support ticket on the Salesforce sync has been open for two weeks now. My team keeps asking me where it stands and I don't have an answer.
[01:22] Sarah Johnson: That's on me — I should have escalated that sooner. I'll get you an update today with a concrete resolution date.
[01:35] Marcus Webb: I'd appreciate that. The other thing — and Dana, you can jump in here — is that we've noticed two of our team members haven't really been using the platform since February.
[01:52] Dana Price: Right. Tom and Alicia. They were supposed to be our power users on the RevOps side. Something changed.
[02:08] Sarah Johnson: Do you know what happened? Was there a role change, or is it more about the workflow not fitting?
[02:19] Dana Price: Honestly, I don't know. I think they tried it for a few weeks and then went back to spreadsheets. That's not a great sign.
[02:35] Marcus Webb: We also missed our last two check-in calls. I know that's partly on us — scheduling got chaotic in February. But it means we haven't had a real conversation in two months.
[02:52] Sarah Johnson: I take responsibility for not pushing harder to get those rescheduled. That's my job.
[03:05] Dana Price: Look, we still see value here. The core workflow Marcus's team uses is working. But we need to see improvement on the support side and figure out what's going on with Tom and Alicia before we get to renewal.
[03:28] Sarah Johnson: That's fair. I want to ask directly — on a scale of 1 to 10, how confident are you feeling about renewing with us in September?
[03:42] Dana Price: Probably a 6. We're not looking around, but we're not locked in either.
[03:52] Marcus Webb: I'd agree with that. If the support ticket gets resolved quickly and we can get Tom and Alicia re-engaged, that number goes up. If we're still having this same conversation in June, it goes down.
[04:10] Sarah Johnson: That's exactly the clarity I needed. Let me propose this: I'll get a written update on the Salesforce ticket to you by end of day. I'll also set up a separate 30-minute call with Tom and Alicia to understand what's blocking them — not a sales call, just listening.
[04:31] Marcus Webb: That would actually be helpful.
[04:38] Sarah Johnson: And I want to do monthly check-ins with a fixed agenda starting April. No more ad hoc. Would you be open to that?
[04:50] Dana Price: Yes. Put it on the calendar.
[04:58] Marcus Webb: One more thing — our NPS dropped from an 8 to a 6 in the last survey. I don't know if you saw that, but I wanted to call it out.
[05:10] Sarah Johnson: I did see it. It's a signal I take seriously. I'm going to make it my job over the next 90 days to get that number back up.
[05:22] Dana Price: Actions matter more than intentions here. Let's see what happens.
[05:30] Sarah Johnson: Agreed. I'll follow up with a written summary of today's commitments by end of day. Thank you both for being direct with me.
